content:
Angular didn’t become a bad framework. A few key decisions, a changing frontend ecosystem, and a powerful network effect helped React take the lead.

5 min read
Just now

--

Press enter or click to view image in full size

Photo by Caspar Camille Rubin on UnsplashI’ve been working with Angular for years, and I still like it.
So this isn’t an article about why Angular is bad.
It isn’t.
In fact, for large applications, especially in enterprise environments, I still think Angular is one of the most complete frontend frameworks available.
But if we’re being honest, something happened over the last decade.
React became the choice most developers gravitated toward.
And I don’t think it happened because React was necessarily a better technology.
I think Angular made a few decisions along the way that gave React the opportunity to take the lead.
And once React started gaining momentum, it became very difficult to catch up.
The first big problem: AngularJS to Angular
I think this was probably the most important moment.
